info.title: TatvaCare Partner API
info.description:
Push leads into TatvaCare and read their state back. Your key decides exactly which fields you can read and write, and which product line your leads belong to — there is no fixed payload.

## What this API does

Create or update leads by phone (idempotent), single or in bulk. Read one or list many. Delete by id. And **discover your schema** at runtime — call `lead_schema` to see exactly what your key may send and read.

## The shape

```
 Lead (one per phone, per product line)
   ├── top-level fields        — first_name, mobile_no, status, …
   └── child tables (arrays)   — e.g. custom_lab_profile[], custom_plan_profile[]
```

A lead is unique per phone within your line — re-sending the same phone updates that lead, it never duplicates, so every write is safe to retry. Your product line is fixed to your key.

## Where to start

- **New here?** Read the [Welcome](/docs/partner-welcome) and [Quickstart](/docs/partner-quickstart).
- **Building a request?** Call [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema) first — it returns your fields, which are required, and how your child tables work.
- **Reading a response?** See [Reading values](/docs/reading-values) and [Errors](/docs/partner-errors).

Every endpoint has a live **try-it** console below — open it, paste your key, and hit **Send**.

tags.name: Getting started
tags.description:
## Getting started

Three steps to your first call:

1. **Get a key** — you'll receive an API key + secret for your product line from TatvaCare Ops.
2. **Set the header** on every request — see **Authentication** below.
3. **Verify + discover** — call **Verify your API key**, then call `lead_schema` to see exactly which fields your key is granted, and build your create/update from that.

There is no fixed payload — your key decides your fields and your line.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
## How to authenticate

Every request needs an `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>
```

Literal lowercase `token`, a single space, then your key and secret joined by a colon. Use **Verify your API key** below to confirm the header is right before any lead call.

tags.name: Partner Lead API
tags.description: Your entire surface — the gated lead methods. Each one resolves what your key is allowed to do: your fixed product line, and the fields you may send and read. Start with `lead_schema` to discover your contract, then create, read, update, or delete. Bulk variants handle up to 100 records per call.

apiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
apiKeyAuth.description: Format: `token <api_key>:<api_secret>` — literal lowercase `token`, single space, key:secret joined by `:`. See [Conventions](/docs/conventions#auth) for how to request a key and verify it.

CRMLeadUniversal.description: Universal fields on `CRM Lead` itself. The top-level fields your key may send/read live here; child tables embed as arrays. Call [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema) to see exactly which fields your key is granted.

name.description: Frappe primary key, autonamed `CRM-LEAD-YYYY-NNNNN`. **Read-only** — never include in POST bodies (server generates).

first_name.description: First name. Set to `(no name)` if you genuinely don't have it (`lead_create` default-fills this if omitted).

mobile_no.description: **Required** — the dedup anchor (with product line + group). E.164 format: `+<country code><digits>`, no hyphens, no spaces.

phone.description: Secondary phone (optional). E.164 same as mobile_no.

custom_alternate_number.description: Alternate contact phone (optional). E.164 same as mobile_no.

status.description: **Required on create.** Universal lifecycle status. New leads start as `New`. Junk and Unqualified are 'Lost' type and require `lost_reason`. Read the allowed values from [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema).

source.description: Lead acquisition channel. Link to `CRM Lead Source`. Read the allowed values from [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema).

lead_owner.description: Email of the CRM user owning this lead. Link to `User`. **Only set if the email is a real Frappe user** — otherwise leave blank to avoid `LinkValidationError`.

lost_reason.description: **Required when `status` is `Junk` or `Unqualified`.** Link to `CRM Lost Reason`.

lost_notes.description: Free-text note explaining why the lead was lost.

custom_vertical.description: **Permission-locked** (`permlevel=1`). Top-level product line. Link to `CRM Vertical`. See [Concepts › routing chain](/docs/discover-schema).

custom_group.description: **Permission-locked.** Sub-line under the vertical. Link to `CRM Group`.

custom_current_program.description: The PRIMARY program. Link to `CRM Program`. **`permlevel=0`** — rep-writable, so a rep can self-handoff a program within their line (only `custom_vertical` + `custom_group` are manager-locked at `permlevel=1`). Multi-program patients are supported via profile-row presence (see [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema)).

custom_previous_program.description: **Permission-locked.** The lead's previous program if they transitioned. Link to `CRM Program`. Captures the LAST switch only — full history is in the (deferred) activity layer.

custom_origin_vertical.description: **Permission-locked. Write-once on capture, never updated.** Records the vertical the patient first entered through, even if their current vertical changes later.

custom_source_origin.description: Where the lead source data originated (deprecated for new writes — use `source` instead).

custom_program_lifecycle_stage.description: **Permission-locked.** Cross-program lifecycle marker (Acquisition / Active / Retention / etc.) — used for cohort analytics.

custom_patient_id.description: External patient identifier from the source system (e.g. MyTatva user ID, partner system ID).

custom_lsq_prospect_id.description: Legacy LeadSquared ProspectId — migration-only dedup key. Not populated for new leads after the LSQ cutover.

custom_lsq_lead_stage_legacy.description: Legacy LeadSquared stage. Read-only for reporting.

custom_lead_gen_id.description: External lead-gen system identifier (campaign or form submission ID).

custom_patient_age.description: Patient age in years. Not auto-calculated from DOB today — caller-provided.

custom_caregiver_relation.description: Caregiver's relationship to patient (e.g. Spouse, Daughter, Son).

custom_plan_profile.description: **Embedded child rows** (max 1 today). Plan / PSP / subscription / drug-program / payment / acquisition / dx-funnel data. On PUT, the array REPLACES the existing rows — see [Write › profile semantics](/docs/api).

custom_lab_profile.description: Embedded child rows. Lab values, vitals, conditions, scores.

custom_care_providers_profile.description: Embedded child rows. Care team — coaches, RM, therapist, doctor, hospital.

modified.description: **Read-only.** Last-modified timestamp. Useful for change-tracking / delta sync.

PlanProfile.description: A child table of the lead holding plan-shaped data: subscription / enrollment, payment + insurer fields, acquisition (UTM + signup), and a program substage. Single-row — your fields merge onto the one row. The exact fields your key may use come from [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema).

signup_date.description: Date the patient signed up (any platform).

mobile_app_signup_date.description: Date specifically of mobile app sign-up.

signed_up_on_app.description: Boolean flag (0/1). Whether the patient signed up via mobile app.

platform.description: Mobile app platform — Android / iOS / Web.

user_source.description: Free-text source identifier (granular than `CRM Lead Source`).

utm_disease.description: UTM disease tag — used in disease-targeted campaigns.

source_campaign.description: Source campaign (alternate naming used by some campaigns).

plan_name.description: Name of the plan / PSP / subscription the patient is enrolled in.

plan_includes_cgm.description: Boolean. Whether the plan includes a Continuous Glucose Monitor.

bca_device.description: Body Composition Analyzer device, if part of the plan.

dx_landing_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient landed on the lab-test booking page. Stamp current time when the LSQ flag fires.

dx_intent_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient indicated intent to book.

dx_details_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient submitted personal details.

dx_address_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient submitted address.

dx_slot_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient selected a slot.

labtest_booked_at.description: Lab test booking completed (terminal funnel step).

invoice_uploaded.description: Boolean. Whether the patient has uploaded an invoice.

payment_link.description: URL the patient can use to complete payment.

paying_status.description: Free-text payment status (e.g. Paid, Pending, Defaulted).

policy_number.description: Insurer policy number (e.g. Niva Bupa policy).

member_id.description: Insurer member identifier within the policy.

physio_or_dietician.description: Boolean. Whether the plan includes physio / dietician.

transition_from_sigrima.description: Transition marker if patient came from Sigrima.

transition_from_ujvira.description: Transition marker if patient came from Ujvira.

transition_old_to_new_pap.description: Transition from old PAP to new PAP.

rx_status.description: Prescription review status (e.g. Pending / Approved / Rejected).

rx_reviewed_by.description: Email of the user who reviewed the prescription. Link to `User`.

rx_rejection_reason.description: Reason the prescription was rejected (if applicable).

substage.description: **Program-scoped operational stage.** Link to `CRM Lead Substage`. Value format is `{program}-{substage_name}` (e.g. `Niva Bupa-Payment Link Sent`, `Sigrima-Patient Ready for Chemo`). Enumerate valid values for a program via [Lookups › Lead Substage](/docs/discover-schema). The universal `status` field on `CRM Lead` is independent of substage — see [Concepts › substages](/docs/discover-schema).

LabProfile.description: A multi-row child table of the lead, one row per lab report (keyed by `report_date`): lab values, vitals, conditions, and metabolic scores. The exact fields your key may use come from [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema).

bmi.description: Body Mass Index. Caller-computed today (no auto-calc hook yet). Formula: `weight_kg / (height_meters^2)`.

primary_condition.description: Patient's primary medical condition. Free text today; will become a Link master when the option list is locked.

comorbid_conditions.description: Comma-separated co-morbid conditions. Will move to a child Table doctype when multi-select pattern is needed.

metabolic_questionnaire_link.description: URL to the metabolic questionnaire PDF / form.

assessment_responses.description: Raw assessment payload — typically a JSON-as-string blob with all questionnaire responses.

report_date.description: Date the lab report was generated (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

CareProvidersProfile.description: A single-row child table of the lead: the patient's care team (coaches, RM, therapist) and medical providers (treating doctor + hospital). The exact fields your key may use come from [`lead_schema`](/docs/discover-schema).

success_coach_name.description: Success Coach name — primary internal contact for the patient.

rm_name.description: Relationship Manager name — handles calls and follow-ups.

abm_name.description: Area Business Manager — operational lead handler for PSP-style engagements.

coach_appointment_booked_at.description: Timestamp when the patient's first coach appointment was booked.

UpsertLeadRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.lead_create`. Only `mobile_no` is strictly required (the dedup anchor); `first_name` and `status` are default-filled if missing (`(no name)` and `New`). All other fields are optional and must be within the fields your key is allowed to send (call `lead_schema` to see them). You never send routing — it's set for you server-side. Profile children embed as arrays and merge field-by-field.

LeadUpdateRequest.description: Request body for `lead_update`. Carries the CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned) plus any allowed fields to change. Parent fields you SEND are updated, fields you OMIT are left alone; profile children merge field-by-field. You can't change routing — it's fixed to your key.

PartnerError.description: Every failure on a partner method returns this exact envelope — including framework-layer errors (bad key, malformed body), which are normalised so you never see a raw traceback. Branch on `error.code` (stable, machine-readable); show `error.message` to a human; the HTTP status mirrors the code. See the **Errors** page for the full `error.code` → HTTP map.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code. Branch on this, not on the message text.

fields.description: Present on some `validation_error`s — the field name(s) that failed.

retry_after.description: Present only on `rate_limited` (429): seconds until your key's budget resets.

PartnerWriteResponse.description: Success envelope from `lead_create` / `lead_update` / `lead_delete`. **Top-level — there is no `message` wrapper** (the gated partner methods emit the body directly). Read `status` first, then `action`, then `data`. A failure instead returns the [PartnerError](#partnererror) envelope with a non-2xx status.

action.description: What happened: a new lead, an upsert onto an existing one, or a delete.

name.description: CRM Lead id (e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`). PUT this back to `lead_update`.

source.description: Lead source (forced from your mapping for partners).

PartnerReadResponse.description: Success envelope from `lead_get` / `lead_list`. Top-level (no `message` wrapper). `action` is `fetched`; `data` carries the curated lead (`lead_get`) or the page object (`lead_list`).

data.description: The curated lead (`lead_get`) or the page object `{total,count,offset,limit,leads[]}` (`lead_list`).

PartnerSchemaResponse.description: Success envelope from `lead_schema`. Top-level (no `message` wrapper), no `action`. `data` holds the fields THIS key may send/read, the child-write rules, dedup grain, bulk limits, and routing mode.

children.description: Per child table: `{multi_row, key_field, fields[]}`. `multi_row:true` ⇒ upsert-by-key on `key_field`.

child_write.description: How to send child arrays + the upsert-by-key rule.

dedup.description: The lead grain (mobile_no + product line + group).

PartnerBulkResultEntry.description: One record's outcome in a bulk response. `status` is `success` or `error` per row. On `success`: `action`+`name` (writes) or `data` (lead_get_bulk). On `error`: the same `error:{code,message}` block as the top-level [PartnerError](#partnererror).

index.description: 0-based position in the request array (results are input-ordered).

action.description: Present on a successful write row.

name.description: CRM Lead id (present on a successful write row).

data.description: Curated lead (present on a successful `lead_get_bulk` row).

error.description: Present on a failed row (its savepoint rolled back).

PartnerBulkResponse.description: Partial-success envelope shared by every bulk method. Top-level (no `message` wrapper). Each record runs in its own savepoint — one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. `summary` is `{total,succeeded,failed}` for writes and `{requested,found,not_found}` for `lead_get_bulk`. Inspect `results[].status` per row; the HTTP status is 200 even when some rows failed.

status.description: `success` — the batch ran (individual rows may have failed).

MinimalUpsert.summary: Minimum viable — just enough to create a lead
MinimalUpsert.description: Only the dedup key (`mobile_no`) plus mandatory `first_name`/`status` (or let them default-fill). No routing, no profiles. Useful for an absolute-minimum smoke test.

UpsertWithOneProfile.summary: Add one profile child — lab values for an insurer lead
UpsertWithOneProfile.description: Now we attach a Lab Profile row. Typical of an insurer pushing a lab-screened lead.

InsurerLabIntakeLead.summary: Full — insurer-screening lead (labs + policy + alt phone)
InsurerLabIntakeLead.description: Realistic shape for an inbound insurer feed. Plan Profile for policy/payment, Lab Profile for the full panel.

OncologyPSPLead.summary: Full — oncology PSP lead (drug program + care team)
OncologyPSPLead.description: Realistic shape for an Anaya Sigrima clinical lead. Plan Profile has drug-specific fields + the program substage; Care & Providers has the assigned coach + doctor + hospital.

WellnessSubscriptionLead.summary: Full — wellness subscription lead (plan + score + care team)
WellnessSubscriptionLead.description: Realistic shape for a MyTatva-style subscription sign-up. Plan Profile carries subscription + UTM; Lab Profile carries metabolic score; Care & Providers carries the assigned coach stack.

UpsertSuccessUpdated.summary: lead_create response — updated (existing lead found on the same line)

LeadSchemaResponse.summary: lead_schema — a key granted labs + plan fields

data.child_write: Send each child as a JSON array under its key. Multi-row children are upsert-by-key on key_field: a new key adds a row, the same key updates that row (only sent fields change), omitted rows are untouched. Single-row children merge onto the one row. Delete a row with {<key_field>, "_delete": true}.
data.dedup: A lead is unique per (mobile_no, product line, group). Re-sending the same trio updates that lead; a different line creates a new one.

routing.note: Your routing is fixed. Any routing fields you send are ignored.

CuratedLeadGet.summary: lead_get — one curated lead with children

LeadUpdateRequest.summary: lead_update — flip status on a known lead

value.name: CRM-LEAD-2026-00123
value.summary: lead_update — flip status, upsert one lab row by its key

LeadListResponse.summary: lead_list — page of curated leads (no children)

get.summary: Discover the fields your key may use
get.description: **Discovery — call this in your integration code instead of hardcoding a field list.** Returns exactly the parent + child fields THIS key may send/read (each with `fieldname`, `label`, `type`, `required`, `options`), the child-write/dedup notes, the bulk/list limits, and your `routing` mode. Two keys get **different** lists — build from what *your* key returns. `routing.mode` is `forced` — your line is fixed to your key.

200.description: The caller's allowed fields + routing mode under `data` (no `message` wrapper).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

get.summary: Read one lead by name or mobile_no
get.description: Fetch ONE lead by `name` **or** `mobile_no`. Returns only the caller's allowed fields plus the read-only routing block, with profile children (`custom_lab_profile`, `custom_plan_profile`) inlined and filtered to allowed fields. You only see leads on your own line; a lookup outside it returns a 404 `not_found`. Pass exactly one of `name` / `mobile_no`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CRM Lead id (the value `lead_create` returned). Use this OR `mobile_no`.

parameters.name: mobile_no
parameters.description: 10-digit Indian number (auto-normalised to `+91…`) or an already-`+`-prefixed number. Use this OR `name`.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call, or the lead is outside your line.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

get.description: List leads on the caller's line, filtered + paginated. Honours a **curated, safe set of filters only** (never arbitrary fields) and always auto-scopes to your line. Returns curated parent fields, **no children** — use `lead_get` for the full record. Page size defaults to 20, max 200; page with `offset`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Rows to skip (paging). Alias `limit_start` is also accepted.

200.description: A page of curated leads under `data` (no children — use `lead_get` for the full record).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

post.description:
**The default write endpoint for ~99% of integrations.** Idempotent on the lead grain (`mobile_no` + product line + group). Safe to retry. Finds the existing lead matching that trio and updates it, or creates a new one. Returns the CRM `name` — PUT it back to `lead_update`. Profile children are passed as embedded arrays.

### What you may send

Only the fields YOUR key is allowed to send — call `lead_schema` to see them. Your routing (`source` / product line / `group` / `program`) is **set for you** and stamped server-side — any routing fields you send are ignored. Fields outside your allowed set are dropped.

### How it merges

For an existing lead, parent fields you SEND are updated; fields you OMIT are LEFT ALONE. Profile children **merge field-by-field** into the existing row (a partial write never wipes the row).

### Default-fill behaviour

- Missing `first_name` → set to `(no name)`
- Missing `status` → set to `New`
- Profile arrays accept either actual JSON arrays or stringified JSON.

### Common pitfalls

- Sending an unknown option value (e.g. a status that isn't valid) → `validation_error`. Read the allowed values from `lead_schema` first.
- Setting `status` to a 'lost' value without its required reason → `validation_error`.
- Sending fields outside your allowed set → silently dropped (not an error). Confirm with `lead_schema`.

### Related

[Read one lead](/docs/api) to fetch the lead back after writing. [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema) to read your fields, required flags, and child keys before writing.

> 🔑 **Discover your fields first.** There's no fixed payload — your granted fields, which are required, and each child table's key all come from **`lead_schema`**. Call it, read `required` / `multi_row` / `key_field`, then build this request. See [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call. (or a partner reached outside their line).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

post.description:
Create-or-update up to **100** leads in one call. Body: `{"leads":[{...}, …]}`. Each record uses the same idempotent phone+line dedup and field rules as `lead_create`, and runs in its own savepoint — **partial success**: one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. Over 100 records returns a clean error (page the rest).

> 🔑 **Discover your fields first.** There's no fixed payload — your granted fields, which are required, and each child table's key all come from **`lead_schema`**. Call it, read `required` / `multi_row` / `key_field`, then build this request. See [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema).

leads.description: Up to 100 lead payloads (same shape as `lead_create`).

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

post.summary: Read many leads by names or mobile_nos
post.description: Read up to **100** leads in one call by `names` **or** `mobile_nos` (send one). Returns the curated leads found (allowed fields + read-only routing + inlined children). Out-of-scope or missing ids are simply omitted — `found` may be less than `requested`. Uses POST because the id list travels in the body.

names.description: Up to 100 CRM Lead ids. Use this OR `mobile_nos`.

mobile_nos.description: Up to 100 phone numbers (auto-normalised). Use this OR `names`.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

put.summary: Update a lead by CRM name
put.description:
Update an existing lead identified by its CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned). Send `name` plus any allowed fields; parent fields you SEND are updated, fields you OMIT are LEFT ALONE, and profile children **merge field-by-field** (a partial write never wipes the row). You can only update leads on your own line, and you can never move a lead to a different line (routing is fixed to your key).

> 🔑 **Discover your fields first.** There's no fixed payload — your granted fields, which are required, and each child table's key all come from **`lead_schema`**. Call it, read `required` / `multi_row` / `key_field`, then build this request. See [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or the lead is outside your line.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

put.description:
Update up to **100** leads in one call. Body: `{"updates":[{"name":…, …fields}, …]}` — each entry needs its CRM `name` plus the fields to change. Same merge semantics and partner scope-check as `lead_update`, applied per record in its own savepoint (**partial success**).

> 🔑 **Discover your fields first.** There's no fixed payload — your granted fields, which are required, and each child table's key all come from **`lead_schema`**. Call it, read `required` / `multi_row` / `key_field`, then build this request. See [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema).

updates.description: Up to 100 update payloads; each carries `name` + allowed fields.

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or a record is outside your line.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

delete.summary: Delete a lead by CRM name
delete.description:
Delete a lead by its CRM `name` (query param or body). You can only delete leads on your own line. A lead with linked activity (calls, tasks, notes) raises `LinkExistsError`, so a worked lead can't be silently nuked.

> 🔑 **Discover your fields first.** There's no fixed payload — your granted fields, which are required, and each child table's key all come from **`lead_schema`**. Call it, read `required` / `multi_row` / `key_field`, then build this request. See [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CRM Lead id to delete (may also be sent in the JSON body).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or the lead is outside your line.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

409.description: Lead has linked activity — `cannot_delete` (generic message by design).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

delete.description:
Delete up to **100** leads by CRM `name`. Body: `{"names":[…]}`. Each delete runs in its own savepoint (**partial success**) and is partner scope-checked; a lead with linked activity fails with `LinkExistsError` for that row while the rest proceed.

> 🔑 **Discover your fields first.** There's no fixed payload — your granted fields, which are required, and each child table's key all come from **`lead_schema`**. Call it, read `required` / `multi_row` / `key_field`, then build this request. See [Discover your schema](/docs/discover-schema).

names.description: Up to 100 CRM Lead ids to delete.

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or a record is outside your line.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

get.description:
The fastest way to confirm your `Authorization` header is correct — it returns the account your key belongs to. Make this your first call when integrating, and a health-check in CI. Anything other than HTTP 200 means your auth is wrong; fix it before any lead call.

_For now this is the shared key-check endpoint; a partner-specific verify endpoint may be added later._
